```{r Export-Leaflet-JIAF-HTML-Index-w-Suitability}
            brks <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)
            # Legend-specific palette with full 0-1 domain ensures color consistency
              legend_pal <- leaflet::colorNumeric(
                    palette  = viridisLite::magma(256),
                    domain   = c(0, 1),
                    na.color = "transparent"
                )
                cols <- legend_pal(brks)
          } else {
              brks <- pretty(domain, n = n)
              brks <- brks[is.finite(brks)]
              cols <- pal(brks)
        }
        
        tags$div(
            id = id,
            class = "map-legend",
            style = paste0(
                "background: rgba(255,255,255,0.92); padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 8px; ",
                "box-shadow: 0 1px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.25); font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.15;"
            ),
            tags$div(style = "font-weight:700; margin-bottom:6px;", title),
            tags$div(
                lapply(seq_along(brks), function(i) {
                    tags$div(
                        style = "display:flex; align-items:center; margin:2px 0;",
                        tags$div(
                            style = paste0(
                                "width:14px;height:14px;border-radius:3px;margin-right:8px;background:",
                                cols[i], ";"
                            )
                        ),
                        tags$div(format(round(brks[i], 1), nsmall = 1))
                    )
                })
            )
        )
    }
    
    # Define color palettes
      sunset_cols <- colorspace::sequential_hcl(256, palette = "Sunset")
    
    # Aggregate ADM3 boundaries to ADM2 (commune) level
      Haiti_adm2_boundaries <- Haiti_adm3 |>
          group_by(ADM2_EN, ADM2_PCODE, ADM1_EN, ADM1_PCODE) |>
          summarise(.groups = "drop")
    
    # Extract zonal statistics for suitability across all warming scenarios
      zonal_stats_adm2 <- exactextractr::exact_extract(
          raster_stack,
          Haiti_adm2_boundaries,
          fun = function(values, coverage_fraction) {
              # Calculate coverage-weighted mean suitability for each scenario
                suitability_means <- apply(
                    values[, 1:5, drop = FALSE],
                    2,
                    \(x) weighted.mean(x, coverage_fraction, na.rm = TRUE)
                )
              
              data.frame(
                  mean_baseline_suitability = suitability_means[1],
                  mean_suitability_1_5C     = suitability_means[2],
                  mean_suitability_2C       = suitability_means[3],
                  mean_suitability_3C       = suitability_means[4],
                  mean_suitability_4C       = suitability_means[5]
              )
          }
      )
    
  # Join suitability metrics with JIAF deprivation indices
    Haiti_adm2_interactive <- Haiti_adm2_boundaries |>
        bind_cols(zonal_stats_adm2) |>
        left_join(
            jiaf_deprivation_primary,
            by = c("ADM2_PCODE" = "admin2_pc")
        ) |>
        mutate(
            # Calculate change in suitability relative to baseline
              suitability_change_1_5C = mean_suitability_1_5C - mean_baseline_suitability,
              suitability_change_2C   = mean_suitability_2C   - mean_baseline_suitability,
              suitability_change_3C   = mean_suitability_3C   - mean_baseline_suitability,
              suitability_change_4C   = mean_suitability_4C   - mean_baseline_suitability,
            # Round numeric values for cleaner popup display
              across(
                  starts_with("mean_"),
                  \(x) round(x, 3)
              ),
              across(
                  starts_with("suitability_change"),
                  \(x) round(x, 3)
              ),
              across(
                  starts_with("index_"),
                  \(x) round(x, 3)
              )
        )
  
  # Create interactive popup HTML with suitability, composite indices, and sector rates
  Haiti_adm2_interactive <- Haiti_adm2_interactive |>
      mutate(
          popup_html = paste0(
              "<div style='font-family: Arial; font-size: 12px; max-width: 380px;'>",
              "<div style='font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 4px;'>", ADM2_EN, "</div>",
              "<div style='color:#444; margin-bottom: 6px;'>", ADM1_EN, " • ", ADM2_PCODE, "</div>",
              "<hr style='margin: 6px 0;'>",
              "<b><i>Aedes aegypti</i> mean suitability</b><br>",
              "Baseline: ", mean_baseline_suitability, "<br>",
              "1.5°C: ", mean_suitability_1_5C, " (",
                  ifelse(suitability_change_1_5C >= 0, "+", ""),
                  suitability_change_1_5C, ")<br>",
              "2°C: ", mean_suitability_2C, " (",
                  ifelse(suitability_change_2C >= 0, "+", ""),
                  suitability_change_2C, ")<br>",
              "3°C: ", mean_suitability_3C, " (",
                  ifelse(suitability_change_3C >= 0, "+", ""),
                  suitability_change_3C, ")<br>",
              "4°C: ", mean_suitability_4C, " (",
                  ifelse(suitability_change_4C >= 0, "+", ""),
                  suitability_change_4C, ")<br>",
              "<hr style='margin: 6px 0;'>",
              "<b>JIAF composite indices</b><br>",
              "Compensatory: <b>",
                  ifelse(is.na(index_comp_wt_norm), "No data", index_comp_wt_norm),
              "</b><br>",
              "Max-dominant: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(index_max_wt_norm), "No data", index_max_wt_norm),
              "<br>",
              "<hr style='margin: 6px 0;'>",
              "<b>JIAF sector deprivation rates</b><br>",
              "CCCM: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(CCCM_rate), "No data", round(CCCM_rate, 3)),
              "<br>",
              "Education: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(Education_rate), "No data", round(Education_rate, 3)),
              "<br>",
              "Nutrition: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(Nutrition_rate), "No data", round(Nutrition_rate, 3)),
              "<br>",
              "Food Security: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(Food.Security_rate), "No data", round(Food.Security_rate, 3)),
              "<br>",
              "Health: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(Health_rate), "No data", round(Health_rate, 3)),
              "<br>",
              "Protection: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(Overarching.Protection_rate), "No data", round(Overarching.Protection_rate, 3)),
              "<br>",
              "Shelter: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(Shelter_rate), "No data", round(Shelter_rate, 3)),
              "<br>",
              "WASH: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(WASH_rate), "No data", round(WASH_rate, 3)),
              "<br>",
              "<hr style='margin: 6px 0;'>",
              "Population: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(population), "No data", scales::comma(population)),
              "<br>",
              "Sector coverage: ",
                  ifelse(is.na(sector_coverage_pct), "No data", paste0(sector_coverage_pct, "%")),
              "</div>"
          )
      )
  
  # Define data domains and color palettes for ADM2
    suit_dom_adm2 <- range(
        c(
            Haiti_adm2_interactive$mean_baseline_suitability,
            Haiti_adm2_interactive$mean_suitability_1_5C,
            Haiti_adm2_interactive$mean_suitability_2C,
            Haiti_adm2_interactive$mean_suitability_3C,
            Haiti_adm2_interactive$mean_suitability_4C
        ),
        na.rm = TRUE
    )
    
    dep_dom_adm2 <- range(
        Haiti_adm2_interactive$index_comp_wt_norm,
        na.rm = TRUE
    )
    
    suitability_pal_adm2 <- leaflet::colorNumeric(
        palette  = viridisLite::magma(256),
        domain   = suit_dom_adm2,
        na.color = "transparent"
    )
    
    deprivation_pal_adm2 <- leaflet::colorNumeric(
        palette  = sunset_cols,
        domain   = dep_dom_adm2,
        na.color = "grey85"
    )
  
  # commune map
  
    commune_map <- leaflet(Haiti_adm2_interactive,
                           options = leafletOptions(preferCanvas = TRUE)) |>
        addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron", group = "Light") |>
        addProviderTiles("OpenStreetMap", group = "OpenStreetMap") |>
        addProviderTiles("Esri.WorldImagery", group = "Satellite") |>
        setView(lng = -72.3, lat = 19.0, zoom = 8) |>
        # Baseline suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm2(mean_baseline_suitability),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1, opacity = 1,
            group = "Baseline",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 2, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~paste0(ADM2_EN, " (", ADM1_EN, ")")
        ) |>
        # 1.5°C warming suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm2(mean_suitability_1_5C),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1, opacity = 1,
            group = "1.5°C Warming",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 2, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~paste0(ADM2_EN, " (", ADM1_EN, ")")
        ) |>
        # 2°C warming suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm2(mean_suitability_2C),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1, opacity = 1,
            group = "2°C Warming",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 2, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~paste0(ADM2_EN, " (", ADM1_EN, ")")
        ) |>
        # 3°C warming suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm2(mean_suitability_3C),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1, opacity = 1,
            group = "3°C Warming",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 2, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~paste0(ADM2_EN, " (", ADM1_EN, ")")
        ) |>
        # 4°C warming suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm2(mean_suitability_4C),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1, opacity = 1,
            group = "4°C Warming",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 2, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~paste0(ADM2_EN, " (", ADM1_EN, ")")
        ) |>
        # JIAF compensatory deprivation index layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~deprivation_pal_adm2(index_comp_wt_norm),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1, opacity = 1,
            group = "Deprivation Index",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 2, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~paste0(
                ADM2_EN, " • JIAF: ",
                ifelse(is.na(index_comp_wt_norm), "No data", index_comp_wt_norm)
            )
        ) |>
        addLayersControl(
            baseGroups = c("Light", "OpenStreetMap", "Satellite"),
            overlayGroups = c(
                "Baseline", "1.5°C Warming", "2°C Warming",
                "3°C Warming", "4°C Warming", "Deprivation Index"
            ),
            options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE),
            position = "topleft"
        ) |>
        hideGroup(c(
            "1.5°C Warming", "2°C Warming",
            "3°C Warming", "4°C Warming", "Deprivation Index"
        )) |>
        # Add suitability legend
        addControl(
            html = legend_control(
                "legend_suit_adm2",
                "Suitability (0.0-0.99)",
                suitability_pal_adm2,
                suit_dom_adm2
            ),
            position = "bottomright"
        ) |>
        # Add deprivation legend
        addControl(
            html = legend_control(
                "legend_dep_adm2",
                "JIAF deprivation (0–1)",
                deprivation_pal_adm2,
                dep_dom_adm2
            ),
            position = "bottomright"
        ) |>
        # JavaScript to toggle legends based on active layer
        htmlwidgets::onRender("
          function(el, x) {
            var suit = document.getElementById('legend_suit_adm2');
            var dep  = document.getElementById('legend_dep_adm2');
            if (dep) dep.style.display = 'none';
    
            function showSuit(){
              if(suit) suit.style.display='block';
              if(dep)  dep.style.display='none';
            }
            function showDep(){
              if(suit) suit.style.display='none';
              if(dep)  dep.style.display='block';
            }
    
            var map = this;
            map.on('overlayadd', function(e) {
              if (!e || !e.name) return;
              if (e.name === 'Deprivation Index') showDep();
              if (e.name === 'Baseline' || e.name.indexOf('Warming') !== -1) showSuit();
            });
            map.on('overlayremove', function(e) {
              if (e && e.name === 'Deprivation Index') showSuit();
            });
          }
        ")

  # Aggregate ADM3 boundaries to ADM1 (department) level
    Haiti_adm1_boundaries <- Haiti_adm3 |>
        group_by(ADM1_EN, ADM1_PCODE) |>
        summarise(.groups = "drop")
  
  # Extract zonal statistics for suitability at department level
    zonal_stats_adm1 <- exactextractr::exact_extract(
        raster_stack,
        Haiti_adm1_boundaries,
        fun = function(values, coverage_fraction) {
            suitability_means <- apply(
                values[, 1:5, drop = FALSE],
                2,
                \(x) weighted.mean(x, coverage_fraction, na.rm = TRUE)
            )
            
            data.frame(
                mean_baseline_suitability = suitability_means[1],
                mean_suitability_1_5C     = suitability_means[2],
                mean_suitability_2C       = suitability_means[3],
                mean_suitability_3C       = suitability_means[4],
                mean_suitability_4C       = suitability_means[5]
            )
        }
    )
    
  # Join suitability metrics with department-level JIAF summary
    Haiti_adm1_interactive <- Haiti_adm1_boundaries |>
        bind_cols(zonal_stats_adm1) |>
        left_join(
            jiaf_dept_summary,
            by = c("ADM1_PCODE" = "admin1_pc")
        ) |>
        mutate(
            suitability_change_1_5C = mean_suitability_1_5C - mean_baseline_suitability,
            suitability_change_2C   = mean_suitability_2C   - mean_baseline_suitability,
            suitability_change_3C   = mean_suitability_3C   - mean_baseline_suitability,
            suitability_change_4C   = mean_suitability_4C   - mean_baseline_suitability,
            across(
                starts_with("mean_"),
                \(x) round(x, 3)
            ),
            across(
                starts_with("suitability_change"),
                \(x) round(x, 3)
            ),
            across(
                ends_with("_mean"),
                \(x) round(x, 3)
            )
        )
  
  # Create interactive popup HTML for departments
    Haiti_adm1_interactive <- Haiti_adm1_interactive |>
        mutate(
            popup_html = paste0(
                "<div style='font-family: Arial; font-size: 12px; max-width: 380px;'>",
                "<div style='font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 4px;'>", ADM1_EN, "</div>",
                "<div style='color:#444; margin-bottom: 6px;'>", ADM1_PCODE, "</div>",
                "<hr style='margin: 6px 0;'>",
                "<b><i>Aedes aegypti</i> suitability</b><br>",
                "Baseline: ", mean_baseline_suitability, "<br>",
                "1.5°C: ", mean_suitability_1_5C, " (",
                    ifelse(suitability_change_1_5C >= 0, "+", ""),
                    suitability_change_1_5C, ")<br>",
                "2°C: ", mean_suitability_2C, " (",
                    ifelse(suitability_change_2C >= 0, "+", ""),
                    suitability_change_2C, ")<br>",
                "3°C: ", mean_suitability_3C, " (",
                    ifelse(suitability_change_3C >= 0, "+", ""),
                    suitability_change_3C, ")<br>",
                "4°C: ", mean_suitability_4C, " (",
                    ifelse(suitability_change_4C >= 0, "+", ""),
                    suitability_change_4C, ")<br>",
                "<hr style='margin: 6px 0;'>",
                "<b>JIAF composite indices</b><br>",
                "Compensatory: <b>",
                    ifelse(is.na(index_comp_wt_mean), "No data", index_comp_wt_mean),
                "</b><br>",
                "Max-dominant: ",
                    ifelse(is.na(index_max_wt_mean), "No data", index_max_wt_mean),
                "<br>",
                "<hr style='margin: 6px 0;'>",
                "Commune count: ",
                    ifelse(is.na(n_communes), "No data", n_communes),
                "<br>",
                "Total population: ",
                    ifelse(is.na(total_population), "No data",
                           scales::comma(total_population)),
                "</div>"
            )
        )
  
  # Define data domains and color palettes for ADM1
    suit_dom_adm1 <- range(
        c(
            Haiti_adm1_interactive$mean_baseline_suitability,
            Haiti_adm1_interactive$mean_suitability_1_5C,
            Haiti_adm1_interactive$mean_suitability_2C,
            Haiti_adm1_interactive$mean_suitability_3C,
            Haiti_adm1_interactive$mean_suitability_4C
        ),
        na.rm = TRUE
    )
    
    dep_dom_adm1 <- range(
        Haiti_adm1_interactive$index_comp_wt_mean,
        na.rm = TRUE
    )
    
    suitability_pal_adm1 <- leaflet::colorNumeric(
        palette  = viridisLite::magma(256),
        domain   = suit_dom_adm1,
        na.color = "transparent"
    )
    
    deprivation_pal_adm1 <- leaflet::colorNumeric(
        palette  = sunset_cols,
        domain   = dep_dom_adm1,
        na.color = "grey85"
    )
    
  # Department map
    department_map <- leaflet(Haiti_adm1_interactive,
                              options = leafletOptions(preferCanvas = TRUE)) |>
        addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron", group = "Light") |>
        addProviderTiles("OpenStreetMap", group = "OpenStreetMap") |>
        addProviderTiles("Esri.WorldImagery", group = "Satellite") |>
        setView(lng = -72.3, lat = 19.0, zoom = 8) |>
      # Baseline suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm1(mean_baseline_suitability),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1.5, opacity = 1,
            group = "Baseline",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 3, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~ADM1_EN
        ) |>
      # 1.5°C warming suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm1(mean_suitability_1_5C),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1.5, opacity = 1,
            group = "1.5°C Warming",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 3, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~ADM1_EN
        ) |>
      # 2°C warming suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm1(mean_suitability_2C),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1.5, opacity = 1,
            group = "2°C Warming",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 3, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~ADM1_EN
        ) |>
      # 3°C warming suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm1(mean_suitability_3C),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1.5, opacity = 1,
            group = "3°C Warming",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 3, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~ADM1_EN
        ) |>
      # 4°C warming suitability layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~suitability_pal_adm1(mean_suitability_4C),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1.5, opacity = 1,
            group = "4°C Warming",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 3, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~ADM1_EN
        ) |>
      # JIAF compensatory deprivation index layer
        addPolygons(
            fillColor = ~deprivation_pal_adm1(index_comp_wt_mean),
            fillOpacity = 0.75, color = "white", weight = 1.5, opacity = 1,
            group = "Deprivation Index",
            highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
                weight = 3, color = "#333", fillOpacity = 0.9, bringToFront = TRUE
            ),
            popup = ~popup_html,
            label = ~paste0(
                ADM1_EN, " • JIAF: ",
                ifelse(is.na(index_comp_wt_mean), "No data", index_comp_wt_mean)
            )
        ) |>
        addLayersControl(
            baseGroups = c("Light", "OpenStreetMap", "Satellite"),
            overlayGroups = c(
                "Baseline", "1.5°C Warming", "2°C Warming",
                "3°C Warming", "4°C Warming", "Deprivation Index"
            ),
            options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE),
            position = "topleft"
        ) |>
        hideGroup(c(
            "1.5°C Warming", "2°C Warming",
            "3°C Warming", "4°C Warming", "Deprivation Index"
        )) |>
      # Add suitability legend
        addControl(
            html = legend_control(
                "legend_suit_adm1",
                "Suitability (mean probability)",
                suitability_pal_adm1,
                suit_dom_adm1
            ),
            position = "bottomright"
        ) |>
      # Add deprivation legend
        addControl(
            html = legend_control(
                "legend_dep_adm1",
                "JIAF deprivation (0–1)",
                deprivation_pal_adm1,
                dep_dom_adm1
            ),
            position = "bottomright"
        ) |>
      # JavaScript to toggle legends based on active layer
        htmlwidgets::onRender("
          function(el, x) {
            var suit = document.getElementById('legend_suit_adm1');
            var dep  = document.getElementById('legend_dep_adm1');
            if (dep) dep.style.display = 'none';
    
            function showSuit(){
              if(suit) suit.style.display='block';
              if(dep)  dep.style.display='none';
            }
            function showDep(){
              if(suit) suit.style.display='none';
              if(dep)  dep.style.display='block';
            }
    
            var map = this;
            map.on('overlayadd', function(e) {
              if (!e || !e.name) return;
              if (e.name === 'Deprivation Index') showDep();
              if (e.name === 'Baseline' || e.name.indexOf('Warming') !== -1) showSuit();
            });
            map.on('overlayremove', function(e) {
              if (e && e.name === 'Deprivation Index') showSuit();
            });
          }
        ")
  
  htmlwidgets::saveWidget(
      commune_map,
      file = file.path(out_dir, "Haiti_Communes_Map.html"),
      selfcontained = TRUE
  )
  
  htmlwidgets::saveWidget(
      department_map,
      file = file.path(out_dir, "haiti_departments_JIAF_suitability.html"),
      selfcontained = TRUE
  )
  
  # Export objects to global environment for further analysis
    assign("haiti_commune_map_jiaf_export", commune_map, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("haiti_department_map_jiaf_export", department_map, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("Haiti_adm2_interactive", Haiti_adm2_interactive, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("Haiti_adm1_interactive", Haiti_adm1_interactive, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  
  commune_map
```

```{r temporal-suitability-change-individual-plots}

  # Line plots of suitability change across warming scenarios for three SDM thresholds, plus JIAF-weighted equivalents
  
    purple_main  <- "#6A1B9A"
    purple_light <- "#9C4DCC"
    purple_dark  <- "#4A148C"

  # Helper: build an ADM2 weight raster from commune polygons
      make_adm2_weight_raster <- function(template_raster, adm2_sf, weight_col) {
        w <- raster::raster(template_raster)
        w[] <- NA_real_
        raster::rasterize(
          x   = as(adm2_sf, "Spatial"),
          y   = w,
          field = adm2_sf[[weight_col]],
          fun   = mean,
          background = NA_real_
        )
      }

  # Core metrics function (unweighted + weighted) for one threshold
    extract_suitability_metrics <- function(individual_predictions,
                                            warming_level,
                                            weight_raster = NULL,
                                            threshold) {
      all_values <- individual_predictions |>
        purrr::map(~ raster::values(.x)) |>
        (\(x) do.call(cbind, x))()
    
      keep <- stats::complete.cases(all_values)
      all_values_clean <- all_values[keep, , drop = FALSE]
    
      mean_suitability_per_model <- colMeans(all_values_clean, na.rm = TRUE)
      prop_above_thr_per_model   <- colMeans(all_values_clean > threshold, na.rm = TRUE)
    
      if (!is.null(weight_raster)) {
        wvec <- raster::values(weight_raster)[keep]
        ok   <- is.finite(wvec) & !is.na(wvec) & wvec > 0
        all_values_w <- all_values_clean[ok, , drop = FALSE]
        wvec         <- wvec[ok]
    
        w_mean_per_model <- apply(
          all_values_w, 2,
          function(x) stats::weighted.mean(x, wvec, na.rm = TRUE)
        )
        w_prop_per_model <- apply(
          all_values_w, 2,
          function(x) stats::weighted.mean(as.numeric(x > threshold), wvec, na.rm = TRUE)
        )
      } else {
        w_mean_per_model <- NA_real_
        w_prop_per_model <- NA_real_
      }
    
      tibble::tibble(
        warming_level = warming_level,
        n_models      = length(individual_predictions),
        threshold     = threshold,
        # Unweighted
        mean_suitability    = mean(mean_suitability_per_model),
        mean_suitability_sd = stats::sd(mean_suitability_per_model),
        mean_suitability_se = mean_suitability_sd / sqrt(length(mean_suitability_per_model)),
        prop_above_thr      = mean(prop_above_thr_per_model),
        prop_above_thr_sd   = stats::sd(prop_above_thr_per_model),
        prop_above_thr_se   = prop_above_thr_sd / sqrt(length(prop_above_thr_per_model)),
        # Weighted
        w_mean_suitability    = if (all(is.na(w_mean_per_model))) NA_real_ else mean(w_mean_per_model),
        w_mean_suitability_sd = if (all(is.na(w_mean_per_model))) NA_real_ else stats::sd(w_mean_per_model),
        w_mean_suitability_se = if (all(is.na(w_mean_per_model))) NA_real_ else w_mean_suitability_sd / sqrt(length(w_mean_per_model)),
        w_prop_above_thr      = if (all(is.na(w_prop_per_model))) NA_real_ else mean(w_prop_per_model),
        w_prop_above_thr_sd   = if (all(is.na(w_prop_per_model))) NA_real_ else stats::sd(w_prop_per_model),
        w_prop_above_thr_se   = if (all(is.na(w_prop_per_model))) NA_real_ else w_prop_above_thr_sd / sqrt(length(w_prop_per_model))
      )
    }

  # Prepare JIAF weight raster (population)
    stopifnot(exists("Haiti_adm2_interactive"))
    stopifnot(all(c("population", "index_geom_wt_norm") %in% names(Haiti_adm2_interactive)))
    stopifnot(exists("baseline_minTP"), exists("baseline_p10"), exists("baseline_maxSSS"))
    
    template_raster <- Aeae_individual_predictions[[1]]
    
    pop_weight_raster <- make_adm2_weight_raster(
      template_raster = template_raster,
      adm2_sf         = Haiti_adm2_interactive,
      weight_col      = "population"
    )

  # Extract metrics for all thresholds and warming levels
    threshold_df <- tibble::tibble(
      threshold_name  = c("minTP", "p10", "maxSSS"),
      threshold_value = c(baseline_minTP, baseline_p10, baseline_maxSSS)
    )

  metrics_all <- threshold_df |>
    mutate(
      metrics = purrr::map2(
        threshold_name, threshold_value,
        \(tname, tval) {
          cat("  Threshold:", tname, "(value =", round(tval, 3), ")\n")
  
          baseline_metrics <- extract_suitability_metrics(
            individual_predictions = Aeae_individual_predictions,
            warming_level          = 0,
            weight_raster          = pop_weight_raster,
            threshold              = tval
          )
  
          warming_metrics <- purrr::map_dfr(
            cmip6_wl_predictions_100pct,
            \(wl_pred) extract_suitability_metrics(
              individual_predictions = wl_pred$individual_predictions,
              warming_level          = wl_pred$warming_level,
              weight_raster          = pop_weight_raster,
              threshold              = tval
            )
          )
  
          dplyr::bind_rows(baseline_metrics, warming_metrics) |>
            dplyr::arrange(warming_level) |>
            dplyr::mutate(
              warming_label = dplyr::if_else(
                warming_level == 0, "Baseline", paste0("+", warming_level, "°C")
              ),
              warming_factor = factor(
                warming_label,
                levels = c("Baseline", "+1.5°C", "+2°C", "+3°C", "+4°C")
              )
            )
        }
      )
    ) |>
    tidyr::unnest(metrics)
  assign("temporal_suitability_metrics_jiaf_multi", metrics_all, envir = .GlobalEnv)

  # For convenience, split into three data frames
    metrics_minTP  <- metrics_all |> filter(threshold_name == "minTP")
    metrics_p10    <- metrics_all |> filter(threshold_name == "p10")
    metrics_maxSSS <- metrics_all |> filter(threshold_name == "maxSSS")

  make_panel_plots <- function(metrics_df, label_prefix, threshold_label) {
    # Mean suitability (unweighted) – same across thresholds, but keep for context
    p_mean <- ggplot(
      metrics_df,
      aes(x = warming_factor, y = mean_suitability, group = 1)
    ) +
      geom_ribbon(
        aes(
          ymin = mean_suitability - mean_suitability_sd,
          ymax = mean_suitability + mean_suitability_sd
        ),
        fill = purple_light, alpha = 0.25
      ) +
      geom_line(color = purple_main, linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_point(
        color = purple_main, size = 4,
        shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 2
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(
        limits = c(0, 1),
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02)),
        breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)
      ) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(label_prefix, " Mean habitat suitability (unweighted)"),
        x = "Global warming level",
        y = "Mean suitability"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
      )
  
    # Unweighted proportion above threshold
    p_prop <- ggplot(
      metrics_df,
      aes(x = warming_factor, y = prop_above_thr * 100, group = 1)
    ) +
      geom_ribbon(
        aes(
          ymin = (prop_above_thr - prop_above_thr_sd) * 100,
          ymax = (prop_above_thr + prop_above_thr_sd) * 100
        ),
        fill = purple_light, alpha = 0.25
      ) +
      geom_line(color = purple_main, linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_point(
        color = purple_main, size = 4,
        shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 2
      ) +
      geom_hline(
        yintercept = 50,
        linetype   = "dashed", color = "grey50", alpha = 0.6
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(
        limits = c(0, 100),
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02)),
        breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)
      ) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(
          label_prefix,
          " Landscape share with suitability ≥ ", threshold_label
        ),
        x = "Global warming level",
        y = "Landscape (%)"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
      )
  
    # Population-weighted mean suitability
    p_w_mean <- ggplot(
      metrics_df,
      aes(x = warming_factor, y = w_mean_suitability, group = 1)
    ) +
      geom_ribbon(
        aes(
          ymin = w_mean_suitability - w_mean_suitability_sd,
          ymax = w_mean_suitability + w_mean_suitability_sd
        ),
        fill = purple_light, alpha = 0.25
      ) +
      geom_line(color = purple_dark, linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_point(
        color = purple_dark, size = 4,
        shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 2
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(
        limits = c(0, 1),
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02)),
        breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)
      ) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(label_prefix, " Mean suitability (population-weighted; JIAF)"),
        x = "Global warming level",
        y = "Pop.-weighted mean suitability"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
      )
  
    # Population-weighted proportion above threshold
      p_w_prop <- ggplot(
        metrics_df,
        aes(x = warming_factor, y = w_prop_above_thr * 100, group = 1)
      ) +
        geom_ribbon(
          aes(
            ymin = (w_prop_above_thr - w_prop_above_thr_sd) * 100,
            ymax = (w_prop_above_thr + w_prop_above_thr_sd) * 100
          ),
          fill = purple_light, alpha = 0.25
        ) +
        geom_line(color = purple_dark, linewidth = 1.2) +
        geom_point(
          color = purple_dark, size = 4,
          shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 2
        ) +
        geom_hline(
          yintercept = 50,
          linetype   = "dashed", color = "grey50", alpha = 0.6
        ) +
        scale_y_continuous(
          limits = c(0, 100),
          expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02)),
          breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)
        ) +
        labs(
          title = paste0(
            label_prefix,
            " Population share in cells with suitability ≥ ", threshold_label
          ),
          x = "Global warming level",
          y = "Pop.-weighted (%)"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
          axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
        )
    
      list(mean = p_mean, prop = p_prop, w_mean = p_w_mean, w_prop = p_w_prop)
    }

  # p10 threshold
    p10_label <- sprintf("%.3f", baseline_p10)
    plots_p10 <- make_panel_plots(metrics_p10, "A) ", p10_label)
  
    combined_temporal_plot_p10 <- cowplot::plot_grid(
      plots_p10$mean,
      plots_p10$prop,
      plots_p10$w_mean,
      plots_p10$w_prop,
      ncol = 2,
      align = "hv"
    )

  assign("plot_temporal_combined_jiaf_p10", combined_temporal_plot_p10, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  assign("plot_temporal_mean_suitability_jiaf_p10", plots_p10$mean, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  assign("plot_temporal_prop_suitable_jiaf_p10", plots_p10$prop, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  assign("plot_temporal_pop_weighted_mean_jiaf_p10", plots_p10$w_mean, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  assign("plot_temporal_pop_weighted_prop_jiaf_p10", plots_p10$w_prop, envir = .GlobalEnv)

  print(combined_temporal_plot_p10)

  # Generate and store minTP and maxSSS panels
    minTP_label   <- sprintf("%.3f", baseline_minTP)
    maxSSS_label  <- sprintf("%.3f", baseline_maxSSS)

  plots_minTP   <- make_panel_plots(metrics_minTP,  "B) ", minTP_label)
  plots_maxSSS  <- make_panel_plots(metrics_maxSSS, "C) ", maxSSS_label)
  
  combined_temporal_plot_minTP <- cowplot::plot_grid(
    plots_minTP$mean,
    plots_minTP$prop,
    plots_minTP$w_mean,
    plots_minTP$w_prop,
    ncol = 2,
    align = "hv"
  )
  
  combined_temporal_plot_maxSSS <- cowplot::plot_grid(
    plots_maxSSS$mean,
    plots_maxSSS$prop,
    plots_maxSSS$w_mean,
    plots_maxSSS$w_prop,
    ncol = 2,
    align = "hv"
  )
  
  assign("plot_temporal_combined_jiaf_minTP", combined_temporal_plot_minTP, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  assign("plot_temporal_combined_jiaf_maxSSS", combined_temporal_plot_maxSSS, envir = .GlobalEnv)
```

```{r temporal-suitability-plots-panel}

# HAITI WARMING METRICS COMPOSITE

  # Colours (match your earlier purple palette)
    purple_main  <- "#6A1B9A"
    purple_dark  <- "#4A148C"
    purple_light <- "#C77DFF"   # ribbon fill (alpha applied below)
    strip_fill   <- "#E1BEE7"
  
  # Scenario order
    scenario_order <- c("Baseline", "+1.5°C", "+2°C", "+3°C", "+4°C")
  
  # Safety: ensure scenario is a factor with the right order
    mean_long <- mean_long %>%
      mutate(scenario = factor(as.character(scenario), levels = scenario_order))
    
    landscape_long <- landscape_long %>%
      mutate(scenario = factor(as.character(scenario), levels = scenario_order))
    
    pop_long <- pop_long %>%
      mutate(scenario = factor(as.character(scenario), levels = scenario_order))
  
  # Strip wrapping helpers
    wrap_strip <- function(x, width = 20) str_wrap(as.character(x), width = width)
  
  # Force the mean strip to be two lines (matches the w-mean strip height)
    mean_strip_labeller <- function(x) {
      x <- as.character(x)
      ifelse(x == "Mean suitability", "Mean\nsuitability", x)
    }
  
  # Shared themes
    base_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      theme(
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    
        strip.text = element_text(size = 11, lineheight = 1.1, margin = margin(3, 4, 3, 4)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = strip_fill, color = "grey70"),
    
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "grey70"),
    
        panel.spacing = unit(0.22, "lines"),
        plot.margin = margin(3, 3, 3, 3, "pt"),
    
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
      )
    
    theme_no_x <- theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    theme_with_x <- theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10))
  
  # Plot builders (single-panel strip via facet_wrap on a 1-level factor)
  
  # 1) Mean suitability (top-left)
    p_mean <- ggplot(
      mean_long %>% filter(metric == "Mean suitability"),
      aes(x = scenario, y = value, group = 1)
    ) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = value - sd, ymax = value + sd),
                  fill = purple_light, alpha = 0.25) +
      geom_line(color = purple_main, linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_point(color = purple_main, size = 2.8) +
      facet_wrap(
        ~ metric, nrow = 1,
        labeller = labeller(metric = mean_strip_labeller)
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1),
                         breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25),
                         expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
      base_theme +
      theme_no_x
  
  # 2) Pop-weighted mean (top-middle)
    p_wmean <- ggplot(
      mean_long %>% filter(metric == "Population-weighted mean suitability"),
      aes(x = scenario, y = value, group = 1)
    ) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = value - sd, ymax = value + sd),
                  fill = purple_light, alpha = 0.25) +
      geom_line(color = purple_dark, linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_point(color = purple_dark, size = 2.8) +
      facet_wrap(
        ~ metric, nrow = 1,
        labeller = labeller(metric = function(x) wrap_strip(x, 28))
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1),
                         breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25),
                         expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
      base_theme +
      theme_no_x +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())  # match earlier style
  
  # 3) % landscape exceeding thresholds (middle row)
    p_land <- ggplot(
      landscape_long,
      aes(x = scenario, y = value, group = 1)
    ) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = value - sd, ymax = value + sd),
                  fill = purple_light, alpha = 0.25) +
      geom_line(color = purple_main, linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_point(color = purple_main, size = 2.8) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", alpha = 0.7, linewidth = 0.5) +
      facet_wrap(
        ~ threshold, nrow = 1,
        labeller = labeller(threshold = function(x) wrap_strip(x, 20))
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),
                         breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
                         expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.03))) +
      base_theme +
      theme_no_x
  
  # 4) % population in landscape exceeding thresholds (bottom row)
    p_pop <- ggplot(
      pop_long,
      aes(x = scenario, y = value, group = 1)
    ) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = value - sd, ymax = value + sd),
                  fill = purple_light, alpha = 0.25) +
      geom_line(color = purple_dark, linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_point(color = purple_dark, size = 2.8) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", alpha = 0.7, linewidth = 0.5) +
      facet_wrap(
        ~ threshold, nrow = 1,
        labeller = labeller(threshold = function(x) wrap_strip(x, 20))
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),
                         breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
                         expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.03))) +
      base_theme +
      theme_with_x
  
  # Assemble with explicit areas (guarantees correct placement)
    blank <- plot_spacer() + theme_void()
    
    lay <- c(
      area(t = 1, l = 1, b = 1, r = 1),  # p_mean
      area(t = 1, l = 2, b = 1, r = 2),  # p_wmean
      area(t = 1, l = 3, b = 1, r = 3),  # blank
      area(t = 2, l = 1, b = 2, r = 3),  # p_land (spans all cols)
      area(t = 3, l = 1, b = 3, r = 3)   # p_pop  (spans all cols)
    )
    
    composite <- p_mean + p_wmean + blank + p_land + p_pop +
      plot_layout(design = lay, widths = c(1, 1, 1), heights = c(1, 1, 1))
    
    print(composite)
```

## Risk Construction

```{r temporal-suitability-tripartite-risk}

  # Tri-partite risk assessment with two JIAF vulnerability indices using baseline SDM thresholds
  
    purple_main  <- "#6A1B9A"
    purple_light <- "#9C4DCC"
    purple_dark  <- "#4A148C"
    
    stopifnot(
      exists("baseline_minTP"),
      exists("baseline_p10"),
      exists("baseline_maxSSS")
    )
  
  # Define all three thresholds
    thresholds <- tibble::tibble(
      threshold_name = c("minTP", "p10", "maxSSS"),
      threshold_value = c(baseline_minTP, baseline_p10, baseline_maxSSS),
      threshold_label = sprintf("%.3f", c(baseline_minTP, baseline_p10, baseline_maxSSS))
    )

  # HELPER FUNCTIONS
  
  # Extract landscape-level metrics (unweighted)
    extract_landscape_metrics <- function(individual_predictions,
                                          warming_level,
                                          threshold) {
      all_values <- individual_predictions |>
        purrr::map(~ raster::values(.x)) |>
        (\(x) do.call(cbind, x))()
    
      keep <- stats::complete.cases(all_values)
      all_values_clean <- all_values[keep, , drop = FALSE]
    
      mean_suitability_per_model <- colMeans(all_values_clean, na.rm = TRUE)
      prop_above_thr_per_model   <- colMeans(all_values_clean > threshold, na.rm = TRUE)
    
      tibble::tibble(
        warming_level = warming_level,
        n_models      = length(individual_predictions),
        mean_suitability    = mean(mean_suitability_per_model),
        mean_suitability_sd = stats::sd(mean_suitability_per_model),
        mean_suitability_se = mean_suitability_sd / sqrt(n_models),
        prop_above_thr    = mean(prop_above_thr_per_model),
        prop_above_thr_sd = stats::sd(prop_above_thr_per_model),
        prop_above_thr_se = prop_above_thr_sd / sqrt(n_models)
      )
    }
  
  # Extract commune-level suitability with SD across models
    extract_commune_suitability <- function(individual_predictions,
                                            adm2_sf,
                                            warming_level) {
      all_extracts <- purrr::map(individual_predictions, function(raster_pred) {
        exactextractr::exact_extract(
          raster_pred,
          adm2_sf,
          fun = "mean",
          progress = FALSE
        )
      })
      extract_matrix <- do.call(cbind, all_extracts)
    
      tibble::tibble(
        ADM2_PCODE       = adm2_sf$ADM2_PCODE,
        warming_level    = warming_level,
        mean_suitability = rowMeans(extract_matrix, na.rm = TRUE),
        sd_suitability   = apply(extract_matrix, 1, stats::sd, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_models         = ncol(extract_matrix)
      )
    }
  
  # Calculate risk indices with percentiles and categories
    calculate_risk_indices <- function(commune_data, vulnerability_data) {
      merged_data <- commune_data |>
        dplyr::left_join(
          vulnerability_data,
          by = c("ADM2_PCODE", "ADM2_EN", "ADM1_EN", "population")
        ) |>
        dplyr::filter(
          !is.na(mean_suitability),
          !is.na(population),
          !is.na(vuln_arith)
        )
    
      risk_data <- merged_data |>
        dplyr::group_by(warming_level) |>
        dplyr::mutate(
          # Within-scenario normalization
            hazard_norm = (mean_suitability - min(mean_suitability, na.rm = TRUE)) /
            (max(mean_suitability, na.rm = TRUE) - min(mean_suitability, na.rm = TRUE)),
          exposure_norm = (population - min(population, na.rm = TRUE)) /
            (max(population, na.rm = TRUE) - min(population, na.rm = TRUE)),
          
          # Risk calculations (two indices)
            risk_arith = hazard_norm * exposure_norm * vuln_arith,
            risk_max   = hazard_norm * exposure_norm * vuln_max,
          
          # Percentile ranks
            risk_arith_percentile = dplyr::percent_rank(risk_arith) * 100,
            risk_max_percentile   = dplyr::percent_rank(risk_max) * 100,
          
          # Risk categories
            risk_arith_category = dplyr::case_when(
              risk_arith >= quantile(risk_arith, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Very High",
              risk_arith >= quantile(risk_arith, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "High",
              risk_arith >= quantile(risk_arith, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Moderate",
              risk_arith >= quantile(risk_arith, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Low",
              TRUE ~ "Very Low"
            ),
            risk_max_category = dplyr::case_when(
              risk_max >= quantile(risk_max, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Very High",
              risk_max >= quantile(risk_max, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "High",
              risk_max >= quantile(risk_max, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Moderate",
              risk_max >= quantile(risk_max, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Low",
              TRUE ~ "Very Low"
            )
        ) |>
        dplyr::ungroup()
    
      risk_data
    }
  
  # SETUP: COMMUNE SUITABILITY (calculated once, used for all thresholds)
  
  stopifnot(exists("Haiti_adm2_interactive"))
  stopifnot(all(c("population", "ADM2_PCODE") %in% names(Haiti_adm2_interactive)))
  
    baseline_commune <- extract_commune_suitability(
      Aeae_individual_predictions,
      Haiti_adm2_interactive,
      warming_level = 0
    )
    
    warming_commune <- purrr::map_dfr(
      cmip6_wl_predictions_100pct,
      \(wl) extract_commune_suitability(
        wl$individual_predictions,
        Haiti_adm2_interactive,
        warming_level = wl$warming_level
      )
    )
    
    all_commune_suit <- dplyr::bind_rows(baseline_commune, warming_commune)
    
    commune_exposure <- all_commune_suit |>
      dplyr::left_join(
        Haiti_adm2_interactive |>
          sf::st_drop_geometry() |>
          dplyr::select(ADM2_PCODE, ADM2_EN, ADM1_EN, population),
        by = "ADM2_PCODE"
      ) |>
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(population), population > 0)
  
  # VULNERABILITY DATA (two indices only)
  
    vulnerability_data <- Haiti_adm2_interactive |>
      sf::st_drop_geometry() |>
      dplyr::select(
        ADM2_PCODE, ADM2_EN, ADM1_EN, population,
        vuln_arith = index_comp_wt_norm,     # Fully compensatory (arithmetic)
        vuln_max   = index_max_wt_norm        # Non-compensatory (maximum)
      ) |>
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(population), population > 0)
  
  # LOOP OVER ALL THREE THRESHOLDS
  
  all_results <- purrr::map(1:nrow(thresholds), function(i) {
    
    thr_name  <- thresholds$threshold_name[i]
    thr_value <- thresholds$threshold_value[i]
    thr_label <- thresholds$threshold_label[i]

    
    # PART 1: LANDSCAPE-LEVEL HAZARD
    
      baseline_landscape <- extract_landscape_metrics(
        Aeae_individual_predictions,
        warming_level = 0,
        threshold = thr_value
      )
      
      warming_landscape <- purrr::map_dfr(
        cmip6_wl_predictions_100pct,
        \(wl) extract_landscape_metrics(
          wl$individual_predictions,
          warming_level = wl$warming_level,
          threshold = thr_value
        )
      )
      
      landscape_metrics <- dplyr::bind_rows(baseline_landscape, warming_landscape) |>
        dplyr::arrange(warming_level) |>
        dplyr::mutate(
          threshold_name = thr_name,
          warming_label = dplyr::if_else(
            warming_level == 0, "Baseline", paste0("+", warming_level, "°C")
          ),
          warming_factor = factor(
            warming_label,
            levels = c("Baseline", "+1.5°C", "+2°C", "+3°C", "+4°C")
          )
        )
    
    # PART 2: POPULATION EXPOSURE
    
      pop_exposure <- commune_exposure |>
        dplyr::group_by(warming_level) |>
        dplyr::summarise(
          total_population = sum(population, na.rm = TRUE),
          pop_weighted_mean_suit = stats::weighted.mean(
            mean_suitability, population, na.rm = TRUE
          ),
          pop_weighted_sd = sqrt(stats::weighted.mean(
            sd_suitability^2, population, na.rm = TRUE
          )),
          pop_in_suitable_areas = sum(
            population[mean_suitability >= thr_value],
            na.rm = TRUE
          ),
          prop_pop_exposed = pop_in_suitable_areas / total_population,
          .groups = "drop"
        ) |>
        dplyr::arrange(warming_level) |>
        dplyr::mutate(
          threshold_name = thr_name,
          warming_label = dplyr::if_else(
            warming_level == 0, "Baseline", paste0("+", warming_level, "°C")
          ),
          warming_factor = factor(
            warming_label,
            levels = c("Baseline", "+1.5°C", "+2°C", "+3°C", "+4°C")
          )
        )
    
    # PART 3: INTEGRATED RISK
    
      risk_data <- calculate_risk_indices(commune_exposure, vulnerability_data)
      
      risk_summaries <- risk_data |>
        dplyr::group_by(warming_level) |>
        dplyr::summarise(
          dplyr::across(
            c(risk_arith, risk_max),
            list(
              mean   = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
              median = ~stats::median(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
              sd     = ~stats::sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
              p90    = ~stats::quantile(.x, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
              p75    = ~stats::quantile(.x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
            ),
            .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"
          ),
          n_communes = dplyr::n(),
          .groups = "drop"
        ) |>
        dplyr::arrange(warming_level) |>
        dplyr::mutate(
          threshold_name = thr_name,
          warming_label = dplyr::if_else(
            warming_level == 0, "Baseline", paste0("+", warming_level, "°C")
          )
        )
    
    # Category distribution
      category_distribution <- risk_data |>
        dplyr::group_by(warming_level) |>
        dplyr::count(risk_arith_category, risk_max_category, .drop = FALSE) |>
        dplyr::ungroup() |>
        dplyr::mutate(threshold_name = thr_name)
      

    # TOP PRIORITY COMMUNES
    
      top_arith <- risk_data |>
        dplyr::select(
          warming_level, ADM2_EN, ADM1_EN,
          risk = risk_arith,
          risk_percentile = risk_arith_percentile,
          risk_category = risk_arith_category,
          hazard_norm, exposure_norm, vuln_arith,
          mean_suitability, population
        ) |>
        dplyr::group_by(warming_level) |>
        dplyr::slice_max(risk, n = 10) |>
        dplyr::mutate(
          rank    = dplyr::row_number(dplyr::desc(risk)),
          variant = "Compensatory"
        ) |>
        dplyr::ungroup()
      
      top_max <- risk_data |>
        dplyr::select(
          warming_level, ADM2_EN, ADM1_EN,
          risk = risk_max,
          risk_percentile = risk_max_percentile,
          risk_category = risk_max_category,
          hazard_norm, exposure_norm, vuln_max,
          mean_suitability, population
        ) |>
        dplyr::group_by(warming_level) |>
        dplyr::slice_max(risk, n = 10) |>
        dplyr::mutate(
          rank    = dplyr::row_number(dplyr::desc(risk)),
          variant = "Non-compensatory"
        ) |>
        dplyr::ungroup()
      
      top_priority <- dplyr::bind_rows(top_arith, top_max) |>
        dplyr::mutate(threshold_name = thr_name)
      
      cat("  Top priority communes extracted\n")
      
      # Return list of results
      list(
        threshold_name = thr_name,
        threshold_value = thr_value,
        threshold_label = thr_label,
        landscape = landscape_metrics,
        exposure = pop_exposure,
        risk_summaries = risk_summaries,
        risk_data = risk_data,
        category_distribution = category_distribution,
        top_priority = top_priority
      )
    })
  
  # COMBINE RESULTS ACROSS THRESHOLDS
  
    landscape_all_thresholds <- purrr::map_dfr(all_results, "landscape")
    exposure_all_thresholds  <- purrr::map_dfr(all_results, "exposure")
    risk_summaries_all       <- purrr::map_dfr(all_results, "risk_summaries")
    risk_data_all            <- purrr::map_dfr(all_results, "risk_data")
    category_dist_all        <- purrr::map_dfr(all_results, "category_distribution")
    top_priority_all         <- purrr::map_dfr(all_results, "top_priority")
    
    # Assign to global environment
    assign("landscape_hazard_metrics_all_thresholds", landscape_all_thresholds, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("population_exposure_metrics_all_thresholds", exposure_all_thresholds, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("risk_summaries_all_thresholds", risk_summaries_all, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("integrated_risk_data_all_thresholds", risk_data_all, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("risk_category_distribution_all_thresholds", category_dist_all, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("top_priority_communes_all_thresholds", top_priority_all, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  
  # KEY FINDINGS (for p10 threshold as primary)
  
    cat("\n=== KEY FINDINGS (p10 threshold) ===\n\n")
    
      p10_results <- all_results[[which(thresholds$threshold_name == "p10")]]
      
      bl <- p10_results$landscape |> dplyr::filter(warming_level == 0)
      hl <- p10_results$landscape |> dplyr::filter(warming_level == max(warming_level))
      be <- p10_results$exposure |> dplyr::filter(warming_level == 0)
      he <- p10_results$exposure |> dplyr::filter(warming_level == max(warming_level))
      br <- p10_results$risk_summaries |> dplyr::filter(warming_level == 0)
      hr <- p10_results$risk_summaries |> dplyr::filter(warming_level == max(warming_level))
      
      cat("1. LANDSCAPE-LEVEL HAZARD:\n")
      cat(sprintf("   Baseline: mean = %.3f (SD = %.3f), %.1f%% of landscape ≥ %s\n",
                  bl$mean_suitability, bl$mean_suitability_sd,
                  bl$prop_above_thr * 100, p10_results$threshold_label))
      cat(sprintf("   Highest:  mean = %.3f (SD = %.3f), %.1f%% of landscape ≥ %s\n",
                  hl$mean_suitability, hl$mean_suitability_sd,
                  hl$prop_above_thr * 100, p10_results$threshold_label))
      cat(sprintf("   Change:   %+.3f in mean, %+.1f pp in high-suitability share\n\n",
                  hl$mean_suitability - bl$mean_suitability,
                  (hl$prop_above_thr - bl$prop_above_thr) * 100))
      
      cat("2. POPULATION EXPOSURE:\n")
      cat(sprintf("   Baseline: mean = %.3f (SD = %.3f), %s people (%.1f%%) ≥ %s\n",
                  be$pop_weighted_mean_suit, be$pop_weighted_sd,
                  scales::comma(be$pop_in_suitable_areas), 
                  be$prop_pop_exposed * 100,
                  p10_results$threshold_label))
      cat(sprintf("   Highest:  mean = %.3f (SD = %.3f), %s people (%.1f%%) ≥ %s\n",
                  he$pop_weighted_mean_suit, he$pop_weighted_sd,
                  scales::comma(he$pop_in_suitable_areas), 
                  he$prop_pop_exposed * 100,
                  p10_results$threshold_label))
      cat(sprintf("   Change:   %+.3f in mean, %+.1f pp in population exposed\n\n",
                  he$pop_weighted_mean_suit - be$pop_weighted_mean_suit,
                  (he$prop_pop_exposed - be$prop_pop_exposed) * 100))
      
      cat("3. INTEGRATED RISK:\n")
      cat(sprintf("   Compensatory:     %.4f -> %.4f (%+.4f) [90th %%ile: %.4f -> %.4f]\n",
                  br$risk_arith_mean, hr$risk_arith_mean,
                  hr$risk_arith_mean - br$risk_arith_mean,
                  br$risk_arith_p90, hr$risk_arith_p90))
      cat(sprintf("   Non-compensatory: %.4f -> %.4f (%+.4f) [90th %%ile: %.4f -> %.4f]\n\n",
                  br$risk_max_mean, hr$risk_max_mean,
                  hr$risk_max_mean - br$risk_max_mean,
                  br$risk_max_p90, hr$risk_max_p90))
    
    # Top commune
      top_baseline_arith <- p10_results$top_priority |>
        dplyr::filter(warming_level == 0, variant == "Compensatory", rank == 1)
      
      cat(sprintf("Top priority commune (Compensatory, baseline): %s (risk = %.4f, %sth percentile)\n",
                  top_baseline_arith$ADM2_EN,
                  top_baseline_arith$risk,
                  round(top_baseline_arith$risk_percentile, 1)))
  
```

```{r risk-data-sina-plots}
  
  # Prepare data
    plot_data <- risk_index_data |>
      mutate(
        warming_label = if_else(
          warming_level == 0, 
          "Baseline", 
          paste0("+", warming_level, "°C")
        ),
        warming_factor = factor(
          warming_label, 
          levels = c("Baseline", "+1.5°C", "+2°C", "+3°C", "+4°C")
        )
      )
  
  # Calculate unified y-limits across both indices
    all_values <- c(plot_data$risk_geom, plot_data$risk_max)
    y_range <- range(all_values, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    base_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      theme(
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 11, margin = margin(r = 8)),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "grey70"),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, 
                                  margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)),
        plot.title.position = "plot",
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        plot.margin = margin(3, 3, 3, 3, "pt")
      )
  
  # Elegant sina plot with line-based summaries
    create_elegant_sina <- function(data, y_var, y_label, title_text, y_limits, hide_y = FALSE) {
      p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = warming_factor, y = .data[[y_var]], 
                            color = warming_factor, fill = warming_factor)) +
        # Sina plot points
        geom_sina(
          alpha = 0.18,
          size = 1.5,
          scale = "width",
          maxwidth = 0.7
        ) +
        # IQR error bars (elegant with end caps)
          stat_summary(
            fun.data = function(x) {
              data.frame(
                y = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
                ymin = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
                ymax = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
              )
            },
            geom = "errorbar",
            width = 0.25,
            linewidth = 1.2,
            alpha = 0.85
          ) +
        # Mean as thick horizontal line
          stat_summary(
            fun = mean,
            geom = "linerange",
            aes(ymin = after_stat(y), ymax = after_stat(y)),
            linewidth = 3.5,
            alpha = 0.9
          ) +
        # Mean point (white center for contrast)
          stat_summary(
            fun = mean,
            geom = "point",
            size = 2.5,
            shape = 21,
            fill = "white",
            stroke = 1.2
          ) +
          scale_y_continuous(
            trans = pseudo_log_trans(sigma = 0.0001, base = 10),
            breaks = c(0, 0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 0.5),
            labels = function(x) format(x, scientific = FALSE, digits = 4),
            limits = y_limits
          ) +
          scale_color_viridis_d(option = "magma", begin = 0.15, end = 0.85) +
          scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "magma", begin = 0.15, end = 0.85) +
          labs(
            y = y_label,
            title = title_text
          ) +
          base_theme +
          annotation_custom(
            grid::rectGrob(
              gp = grid::gpar(fill = "#E1BEE7", col = "grey70", lwd = 0.5)
            ),
            xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = Inf, ymax = Inf
          )
      
      # Hide y-axis text if requested
        if (hide_y) {
          p <- p + theme(
            axis.text.y = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
            axis.title.y = element_blank()
          )
        }
      
      return(p)
    }
  
  # Create both panels with unified scales
    p_geom_sina <- create_elegant_sina(
      plot_data,
      "risk_geom",
      "Risk Index (pseudo-log scale)",
      "Risk Index (Fully Compensatory)\nMean & IQR",
      y_limits = y_range,
      hide_y = FALSE
    )
    
    p_max_sina <- create_elegant_sina(
      plot_data,
      "risk_max",
      "Risk Index (pseudo-log scale)",
      "Risk Index (Non-Compensatory)\nMean & IQR",
      y_limits = y_range,
      hide_y = TRUE  # Hide y-axis for second panel
    )
    
    risk_magma_sina <- p_geom_sina + p_max_sina +
      plot_layout(ncol = 2, widths = c(1, 1))
    
    assign("risk_magma_sina", risk_magma_sina, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    print(risk_magma_sina)
  
  # Save high-resolution outputs
    output_path <- "Replace with your personal (or impersonal) directory"
  
  # Save as PNG (high resolution)
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(output_path, "risk_indices_sina_plot.png"),
      plot = risk_magma_sina,
      width = 10,
      height = 6,
      dpi = 600,
      bg = "white"
    )
  
  # Save as TIFF (high resolution)
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(output_path, "risk_indices_sina_plot.tiff"),
      plot = risk_magma_sina,
      width = 10,
      height = 6,
      dpi = 600,
      bg = "white",
      compression = "lzw"
    )
  
```

```{r supplemental-tables}

# Create department and commune-level summary tables with 8-sector JIAF 


# SECTION 1 — SETUP + RASTER STACK STANDARDISATION + ZONAL STATS FUNCTION

  output_dir <- "replace w/ your output directory if you want to save stuff"
  if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

  stopifnot(exists("Haiti_adm3"))
  stopifnot(exists("raster_stack"))
  stopifnot(exists("jiaf_deprivation_primary"))
  stopifnot(exists("integrated_risk_data_all_thresholds"))
  stopifnot(
      exists("baseline_minTP"),
      exists("baseline_p10"),
      exists("baseline_maxSSS")
  )

  minTP_label  <- sprintf("%.3f", baseline_minTP)
  p10_label    <- sprintf("%.3f", baseline_p10)
  maxSSS_label <- sprintf("%.3f", baseline_maxSSS)

  rs_names <- names(raster_stack)
  
  name_map <- list(
      baseline_suitability = c("baseline_suitability", "baseline", "base", "current"),
      suitability_1_5C     = c("suitability_1_5C", "wl15", "suitability_1.5C", "suitability_15C", "1.5C", "1_5C"),
      suitability_2C       = c("suitability_2C", "wl2", "2C", "2_0C"),
      suitability_3C       = c("suitability_3C", "wl3", "3C", "3_0C"),
      suitability_4C       = c("suitability_4C", "wl4", "4C", "4_0C")
  )
  
  pick_layer <- function(target, candidates, available) {
      hit <- candidates[candidates %in% available]
      if (length(hit) == 0) return(NA_character_)
      hit[[1]]
  }
  
  picked <- vapply(
      names(name_map),
      \(tgt) pick_layer(tgt, name_map[[tgt]], rs_names),
      FUN.VALUE = character(1)
  )
  
  if (anyNA(picked)) {
      missing_targets <- names(picked)[is.na(picked)]
      stop(
          "Could not find required layer(s) in raster_stack: ",
          paste(missing_targets, collapse = ", "),
          "\nAvailable raster_stack names are: ",
          paste(rs_names, collapse = ", "),
          "\nFix by renaming raster_stack layers or extending name_map in this chunk."
      )
  }

  raster_stack_suit <- raster::stack(
      raster_stack[[picked["baseline_suitability"]]],
      raster_stack[[picked["suitability_1_5C"]]],
      raster_stack[[picked["suitability_2C"]]],
      raster_stack[[picked["suitability_3C"]]],
      raster_stack[[picked["suitability_4C"]]]
  )
  
  names(raster_stack_suit) <- c(
      "baseline_suitability",
      "suitability_1_5C",
      "suitability_2C",
      "suitability_3C",
      "suitability_4C"
  )

# ZONAL STATS FUNCTION (cumulative threshold proportions) 
  zonal_stats_fun <- function(values,
                                                          coverage_fraction,
                                                          thr_minTP  = baseline_minTP,
                                                          thr_p10    = baseline_p10,
                                                          thr_maxSSS = baseline_maxSSS) {
      
      # Mean suitability for each scenario layer (coverage-fraction weighted)
        suitability_means <- apply(
            values[, 1:5, drop = FALSE],
            2,
            \(x) weighted.mean(x, coverage_fraction, na.rm = TRUE)
        )
      
      # Proportion of pixels >= threshold (cumulative), weighted by coverage fraction
        prop_ge_thr <- function(thr) {
            apply(
                values[, 1:5, drop = FALSE],
                2,
                \(x) {
                    valid <- is.finite(x) & !is.na(x) &
                        is.finite(coverage_fraction) & !is.na(coverage_fraction)
                    
                    if (sum(valid) == 0) return(NA_real_)
                    
                    sum((x[valid] >= thr) * coverage_fraction[valid], na.rm = TRUE) /
                        sum(coverage_fraction[valid], na.rm = TRUE)
                }
            )
        }
      
      # Proportion of pixels < threshold (absent), weighted by coverage fraction
        prop_lt_thr <- function(thr) {
            apply(
                values[, 1:5, drop = FALSE],
                2,
                \(x) {
                    valid <- is.finite(x) & !is.na(x) &
                        is.finite(coverage_fraction) & !is.na(coverage_fraction)
                    
                    if (sum(valid) == 0) return(NA_real_)
                    
                    sum((x[valid] < thr) * coverage_fraction[valid], na.rm = TRUE) /
                        sum(coverage_fraction[valid], na.rm = TRUE)
                }
            )
        }
      
        props_minTP  <- prop_ge_thr(thr_minTP)
        props_p10    <- prop_ge_thr(thr_p10)
        props_maxSSS <- prop_ge_thr(thr_maxSSS)
        props_absent <- prop_lt_thr(thr_minTP)
        
        data.frame(
            mean_baseline = suitability_means[1],
            mean_wl15     = suitability_means[2],
            mean_wl2      = suitability_means[3],
            mean_wl3      = suitability_means[4],
            mean_wl4      = suitability_means[5],
            
            prop_absent_baseline = props_absent[1],
            prop_absent_wl15     = props_absent[2],
            prop_absent_wl2      = props_absent[3],
            prop_absent_wl3      = props_absent[4],
            prop_absent_wl4      = props_absent[5],
            
            prop_minTP_baseline = props_minTP[1],
            prop_minTP_wl15     = props_minTP[2],
            prop_minTP_wl2      = props_minTP[3],
            prop_minTP_wl3      = props_minTP[4],
            prop_minTP_wl4      = props_minTP[5],
            
            prop_p10_baseline = props_p10[1],
            prop_p10_wl15     = props_p10[2],
            prop_p10_wl2      = props_p10[3],
            prop_p10_wl3      = props_p10[4],
            prop_p10_wl4      = props_p10[5],
            
            prop_maxSSS_baseline = props_maxSSS[1],
            prop_maxSSS_wl15     = props_maxSSS[2],
            prop_maxSSS_wl2      = props_maxSSS[3],
            prop_maxSSS_wl3      = props_maxSSS[4],
            prop_maxSSS_wl4      = props_maxSSS[5]
        )
    }

# SECTION 2 — RISK DATA (DEDUP + PIVOT) + ADM2/ADM1 GEOMS + ZONAL EXTRACTS
  risk_data_wide <- integrated_risk_data_all_thresholds |>
      dplyr::filter(warming_level %in% c(0, 1.5, 2, 3, 4)) |>
      dplyr::distinct(ADM2_PCODE, warming_level, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
      dplyr::select(
          ADM2_PCODE, warming_level,
          risk_arith, risk_arith_percentile, risk_arith_category,
          risk_max,   risk_max_percentile,   risk_max_category
      ) |>
      tidyr::pivot_wider(
          names_from  = warming_level,
          values_from = c(
              risk_arith, risk_arith_percentile, risk_arith_category,
              risk_max,   risk_max_percentile,   risk_max_category
          ),
          names_glue = "{.value}_wl{warming_level}"
      ) |>
      dplyr::rename_with(
          ~ stringr::str_replace(.x, "_wl0$", "_baseline"),
          dplyr::starts_with("risk_")
      )

  # ADM2 / ADM1 geometries
    Haiti_adm2 <- Haiti_adm3 |>
        dplyr::group_by(ADM1_EN, ADM1_PCODE, ADM2_EN, ADM2_PCODE) |>
        dplyr::summarise(.groups = "drop") |>
        sf::st_as_sf()
    
    Haiti_adm1 <- Haiti_adm3 |>
        dplyr::group_by(ADM1_EN, ADM1_PCODE) |>
        dplyr::summarise(.groups = "drop") |>
        sf::st_as_sf()

  # Zonal extracts
    commune_zonal_stats <- exactextractr::exact_extract(
        raster_stack_suit,
        Haiti_adm2,
        zonal_stats_fun
    )
    
    dept_zonal_stats <- exactextractr::exact_extract(
        raster_stack_suit,
        Haiti_adm1,
        zonal_stats_fun
    )

# SECTION 4 — FINAL COLUMN ORDER + EXPORT CSVS + ASSIGN GLOBALS
  commune_table_out <- commune_table |>
      dplyr::select(
          Department = ADM1_EN,
          ADM1_PCODE,
          Commune = ADM2_EN,
          ADM2_PCODE,
          Population = population,
          Data_Quality = data_quality,
          Sector_Coverage_Pct = sector_coverage_pct,
          
          Deprivation_Index_Comp = index_comp_wt_norm,
          Deprivation_Index_Max  = index_max_wt_norm,
          
          Baseline_Mean       = mean_baseline,
          Baseline_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_baseline,
          Baseline_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_baseline,
          Baseline_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_baseline,
          Baseline_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_baseline,
          Baseline_Risk_Comp      = risk_arith_baseline,
          Baseline_Risk_Comp_Pct  = risk_arith_percentile_baseline,
          Baseline_Risk_Comp_Cat  = risk_arith_category_baseline,
          Baseline_Risk_Max       = risk_max_baseline,
          Baseline_Risk_Max_Pct   = risk_max_percentile_baseline,
          Baseline_Risk_Max_Cat   = risk_max_category_baseline,
          
          WL15_Mean       = mean_wl15,
          WL15_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_wl15,
          WL15_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_wl15,
          WL15_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_wl15,
          WL15_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_wl15,
          WL15_Risk_Comp      = `risk_arith_wl1.5`,
          WL15_Risk_Comp_Pct  = `risk_arith_percentile_wl1.5`,
          WL15_Risk_Comp_Cat  = `risk_arith_category_wl1.5`,
          WL15_Risk_Max       = `risk_max_wl1.5`,
          WL15_Risk_Max_Pct   = `risk_max_percentile_wl1.5`,
          WL15_Risk_Max_Cat   = `risk_max_category_wl1.5`,
          
          WL2_Mean       = mean_wl2,
          WL2_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_wl2,
          WL2_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_wl2,
          WL2_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_wl2,
          WL2_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_wl2,
          WL2_Risk_Comp      = risk_arith_wl2,
          WL2_Risk_Comp_Pct  = risk_arith_percentile_wl2,
          WL2_Risk_Comp_Cat  = risk_arith_category_wl2,
          WL2_Risk_Max       = risk_max_wl2,
          WL2_Risk_Max_Pct   = risk_max_percentile_wl2,
          WL2_Risk_Max_Cat   = risk_max_category_wl2,
          
          WL3_Mean       = mean_wl3,
          WL3_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_wl3,
          WL3_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_wl3,
          WL3_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_wl3,
          WL3_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_wl3,
          WL3_Risk_Comp      = risk_arith_wl3,
          WL3_Risk_Comp_Pct  = risk_arith_percentile_wl3,
          WL3_Risk_Comp_Cat  = risk_arith_category_wl3,
          WL3_Risk_Max       = risk_max_wl3,
          WL3_Risk_Max_Pct   = risk_max_percentile_wl3,
          WL3_Risk_Max_Cat   = risk_max_category_wl3,
          
          WL4_Mean       = mean_wl4,
          WL4_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_wl4,
          WL4_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_wl4,
          WL4_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_wl4,
          WL4_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_wl4,
          WL4_Risk_Comp      = risk_arith_wl4,
          WL4_Risk_Comp_Pct  = risk_arith_percentile_wl4,
          WL4_Risk_Comp_Cat  = risk_arith_category_wl4,
          WL4_Risk_Max       = risk_max_wl4,
          WL4_Risk_Max_Pct   = risk_max_percentile_wl4,
          WL4_Risk_Max_Cat   = risk_max_category_wl4
      )
  
  dept_table_out <- dept_table |>
      dplyr::select(
          Department = ADM1_EN,
          ADM1_PCODE,
          Population = total_population,
          
          Deprivation_Index_Comp = index_comp_wt_mean,
          Deprivation_Index_Max  = index_max_wt_mean,
          
          Baseline_Mean       = mean_baseline,
          Baseline_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_baseline,
          Baseline_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_baseline,
          Baseline_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_baseline,
          Baseline_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_baseline,
          Baseline_Risk_Comp  = risk_arith_baseline,
          Baseline_Risk_Max   = risk_max_baseline,
          
          WL15_Mean       = mean_wl15,
          WL15_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_wl15,
          WL15_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_wl15,
          WL15_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_wl15,
          WL15_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_wl15,
          WL15_Risk_Comp  = `risk_arith_wl1.5`,
          WL15_Risk_Max   = `risk_max_wl1.5`,
          
          WL2_Mean       = mean_wl2,
          WL2_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_wl2,
          WL2_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_wl2,
          WL2_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_wl2,
          WL2_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_wl2,
          WL2_Risk_Comp  = risk_arith_wl2,
          WL2_Risk_Max   = risk_max_wl2,
          
          WL3_Mean       = mean_wl3,
          WL3_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_wl3,
          WL3_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_wl3,
          WL3_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_wl3,
          WL3_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_wl3,
          WL3_Risk_Comp  = risk_arith_wl3,
          WL3_Risk_Max   = risk_max_wl3,
          
          WL4_Mean       = mean_wl4,
          WL4_Absent_Pct = prop_absent_wl4,
          WL4_MinTP_Pct  = prop_minTP_wl4,
          WL4_P10_Pct    = prop_p10_wl4,
          WL4_MaxSSS_Pct = prop_maxSSS_wl4,
          WL4_Risk_Comp  = risk_arith_wl4,
          WL4_Risk_Max   = risk_max_wl4
      )
  
  write.csv(
      dept_table_out,
      file.path(output_dir, "Table_Department_Suitability_JIAF_Risk_Summary_8sector.csv"),
      row.names = FALSE
  )

  write.csv(
      commune_table_out,
      file.path(output_dir, "Table_Commune_Suitability_JIAF_Risk_Summary_8sector.csv"),
      row.names = FALSE
  )
  
  assign("dept_summary_table_jiaf",    dept_table_out,    envir = .GlobalEnv)
  assign("commune_summary_table_jiaf", commune_table_out, envir = .GlobalEnv)

```

## Final Comprehensive Risk HTML

```{r Export-Leaflet-Risk-Map-HTML}

# Export Leaflet Risk Map with Risk Index Percentile Layers (Commune Level)

  # Define output directory for HTML exports
    out_dir <- "swap this with your directory, you can't use mine, obviously :)"
      
    if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) {
      dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    }

  # Helper function to extract first layer from RasterBrick objects
    pick_raster <- function(x) {
      if (inherits(x, "RasterBrick")) x[[1]] else x
    }

  # Extract individual raster layers from ensemble predictions
    baseline_raster <- pick_raster(Aeae_ensemble_mean)
    wl15_raster     <- pick_raster(Aeae_WL1_5_suitability_mean)
    wl2_raster      <- pick_raster(Aeae_WL2_suitability_mean)
    wl3_raster      <- pick_raster(Aeae_WL3_suitability_mean)
    wl4_raster      <- pick_raster(Aeae_WL4_suitability_mean)

  # Stack rasters for efficient zonal extraction
    raster_stack <- raster::stack(
      baseline_raster,
      wl15_raster,
      wl2_raster,
      wl3_raster,
      wl4_raster
    )
    names(raster_stack) <- c("baseline", "wl15", "wl2", "wl3", "wl4")

  # Aggregate ADM3 boundaries to ADM2 (commune) level
    Haiti_adm2_boundaries <- Haiti_adm3 |>
      group_by(ADM2_EN, ADM2_PCODE, ADM1_EN, ADM1_PCODE) |>
      summarise(.groups = "drop")

  # Extract zonal statistics for suitability across all warming scenarios
    zonal_stats_adm2 <- exactextractr::exact_extract(
      raster_stack,
      Haiti_adm2_boundaries,
      fun = function(values, coverage_fraction) {
        suitability_means <- apply(
          values[, 1:5, drop = FALSE],
          2,
          \(x) weighted.mean(x, coverage_fraction, na.rm = TRUE)
        )
        
        data.frame(
          mean_baseline_suitability = suitability_means[1],
          mean_suitability_1_5C     = suitability_means[2],
          mean_suitability_2C       = suitability_means[3],
          mean_suitability_3C       = suitability_means[4],
          mean_suitability_4C       = suitability_means[5]
        )
      }
    )

  # Join suitability metrics with JIAF deprivation indices
```
